content_markdown:
It is suggested that the solar power incident at a point on the Earth’s surface depends on

I. daily variations in the Sun’s power output
II. the location of the point
III. the cloud cover at the point.

Which suggestion(s) is/are correct?

diagram_metadata.description: No diagram provided in the image.

vibe_explanation: The solar power reaching a specific point on Earth is influenced by several factors: (I) The Sun's total power output (solar constant) can vary slightly; (II) The location (latitude) determines the angle of incidence and the thickness of the atmosphere the light must pass through; and (III) Cloud cover reflects and absorbs incoming radiation, significantly affecting the power that actually reaches the surface. Therefore, all three factors are correct.
